title: podman kube play returns an error code but creates a pod anyway when pod refers to a non-existent ConfigMap.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman kube play` returns an error code but creates a pod anyway when pod refers to a non-existent ConfigMap.

I'm not certain that this is actually a bug but it feels like unintuitive behavior.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create kube.yaml with contents:
```
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.3.1
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.kubernetes.cri-o.TTY/sleeping-alpine: "false"
    io.podman.annotations.autoremove/sleeping-alpine: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.init/sleeping-alpine: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.privileged/sleeping-alpine: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.publish-all/sleeping-alpine: "FALSE"
  creationTimestamp: "2022-12-21T13:35:12Z"
  labels:
    app: sleeping-alpine-pod
  name: sleeping-alpine-pod
spec:
  automountServiceAccountToken: false
  containers:
  - command:
    - sleep
    - inf
    image: docker.io/library/alpine:latest
    name: sleeping-alpine
    envFrom:
      - configMapRef:
          name: this-does-not-exist
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
        - CAP_NET_RAW
        - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE
  enableServiceLinks: false
```
2. Run `podman kube play ./kube.yaml`
3. Check the existing pods: `podman pod ps`

**Describe the results you received:**
The above steps result in return value 125 from podman and error message "Error: configmap this-does-not-exist not found".
However, when checking the existing pods afterwards, the pod has been created (not running).

**Describe the results you expected:**
No pods were created

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Similar thing happens with Podman API.

I'm not exactly sure if this is an issue or not as the pod is not started but it is not intuitive behavior of the command.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.3.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

Yes (I did not build from main, but used the latest release)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VirtualBox

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out!  @umohnani8, you are working on that, right?
Yup, this was fixed upstream in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16750. It should be available in the next release.
